commands.review_summary:
The PR makes trusted-policy helpers accept an explicit registry and passes `getGlobalPluginRegistry()` into before-tool-call diagnostics and policy execution, with a regression test for active-registry replacement.

PR surface: Source +11, Tests +46. Total +57 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows the existing loader test state where `getGlobalPluginRegistry()` points at the later default registry while the global runner still sees pinned gateway hooks; this PR would inspect only that later registry for trusted policies.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The PR has a concrete trust-boundary finding, but it is maintainer-labeled and needs owner review of the registry-composition fix shape before merge.

target.title: fix: keep trusted policies with hook registry
target.body:
## Summary
- Fix `before_tool_call` trusted-policy lookup so it uses the same plugin registry snapshot as the global hook runner when one exists.
- Let trusted-policy helpers read an explicit registry while preserving the active-registry fallback for callers without a global hook registry.
- Add a regression test for the split-registry state where the Gateway hook registry still has a trusted policy but the later active registry does not.

## What Changed
A Gateway run can preserve the global hook runner from one plugin registry while a later scoped/default registry becomes the active registry. Before this change, tool-call hooks were checked through the global hook runner, but trusted policies were checked through the active registry.

That could produce this state for one tool call:

```text
Gateway/global hook registry:
  before_tool_call hook: yes
  trusted policy: atl-sec-core

Later active registry:
  trusted policies: []
```

The old path could run the plugin hook while skipping the trusted policy. The fixed path resolves a `policyRegistry` from `getGlobalPluginRegistry()` and passes that same registry into `hasTrustedToolPolicies(...)`, `getTrustedToolPolicyDiagnosticEntries(...)`, and `runTrustedToolPolicies(...)`.

## Behavior Addressed
Tool calls that can still reach plugin `before_tool_call` hooks now evaluate trusted tool policies from the same global hook registry before the ordinary hook path runs.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: split-registry trusted-policy bypass in `runBeforeToolCallHook(...)`.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw worktree on current `origin/main` after rebasing branch `jesse/fix-trusted-policy-registry`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.embedded-mode.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src.tsbuildinfo
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.embedded-mode.test.ts: 1 file passed, 12 tests passed
src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts: 1 file passed, 62 tests passed
core tsgo: passed
source-test tsgo: passed
git diff --check: passed
```

Observed result after fix: the new regression test creates a Gateway registry with a trusted policy, replaces the active registry with an empty registry, and confirms the trusted policy still blocks before the ordinary hook runs.

What was not tested: a rebuilt ASSP/RovoClaw container using this patched OpenClaw commit was not run after the code change. The earlier local ASSP debug run reproduced the callback-yes/policy-no symptom before this OpenClaw patch.

## Verification
```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.embedded-mode.test.ts
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
passed

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src.tsbuildinfo
passed

git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
passed
```
